title: phaius woodfordii
intro: Phaius woodfordii, also known as the Woodford's orchid or grass orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Australia. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its roots and rhizomes containing compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Phaius woodfordii include the potential relief of various symptoms such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the roots of Phaius woodfordii are prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety as a treatment for human diseases.
